pairs.question: What is the current name of the arena in San Antonio that serves as the home for the San Antonio Spurs?
pairs.answer: The current name of the arena is the Frost Bank Center.

pairs.question: Which teams have called the arena in San Antonio their home venue, and during what years did they play there?
pairs.answer: The San Antonio Spurs (NBA) have been the primary tenants since 2002. The San Antonio Silver Stars/Stars (WNBA) played there from 2003–2014 and 2016–2017, while the San Antonio Rampage (AHL) played there from 2002–2020.

pairs.question: When did the arena first open, and what was its original name?
pairs.answer: The arena opened on October 18, 2002, under the name SBC Center after SBC Communications, Inc. purchased the naming rights in a 20-year, $41 million deal.

pairs.question: How many years did it take from the start of construction to the Spurs' first game at the arena?
pairs.answer: Construction began on August 24, 2000, and the Spurs played their first game on November 1, 2002. This means it took approximately 2 years and 2 months from the start of construction to the first game.

pairs.question: List the professional wrestling events hosted at the arena and their respective years.
pairs.answer: The arena has hosted several WWE events, including Royal Rumble in 2007, TLC: Tables, Ladders & Chairs in 2009, Vengeance in 2011, and Hell in a Cell in 2018. It also hosted an AEW Collision episode on December 23, 2023.

pairs.question: Write a Python function that takes a year as input and returns the name of the arena in San Antonio for that year.
What was the score of the Spurs' first game at the arena in 2002?
pairs.answer: The Spurs defeated the Toronto Raptors 91–72 in their first game at the arena on November 1, 2002.
